publishedAbstract: + OpenShift console shows one of the below messages on one or more OpenShift nodes (check-out the attached console's screenshots): ~~~ The total CPU resource limit and amount requested by all pods on this node is approaching the node's capacity, Pod performance may be throttled under high load, and new pods may not be schedulable ~~~ ~~~ The total memory resource limit of all pods on this node is approaching the node's capacity, Pods may be terminated if the limite is reached under high load. ~~~ ~~~ This node’s CPU resources are overcommitted. The total CPU resource limit of all pods exceeds the node’s total capacity. The total CPU requested is also approaching the node’s capacity. Pod performance will be throttled under high load, and new pods may not be scheduled on this node. ~~~![screen3](https://access.redhat.com/sites/default/files/attachments/console_error-limit-overcommitted.png)
publishedTitle: Receiving a CPU or memory "resource limit" is "approaching"  or "overcommitted" message on OpenShift 4 Console
